title: Phaseolus trilobatus
taxonomy: Phaseolus trilobatus is a species of wild bean native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the family Fabaceae and is a member of the tribe Phaseoleae. Within the genus Phaseolus, P. trilobatus is closely related to other species of wild and domesticated beans. The genus Phaseolus is part of the larger subfamily Faboideae, which includes a wide range of legumes. As a member of the Fabaceae family, P. trilobatus shares characteristics with other legumes, such as nodulated roots that enable nitrogen fixation.
uses: Phaseolus trilobatus, also known as Three-lobed bean or wild mescal bean, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes for its medicinal properties. The plant contains a compound called munjistin, which has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. In traditional medicine, the plant was used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, sore eyes, and skin conditions. The plant's seeds were also used as a treatment for various health issues, including rheumatism and snake bites. While the plant's medicinal properties have been reported, further research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
benefits: Phaseolus trilobatus, a wild bean native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, is said to have several health benefits. The plant is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect against cell damage and reduce inflammation in the body. Its leaves and pods are also a good source of fiber, which can aid in digestion and promote a healthy gut. The plant's nutritional value and potential health benefits make it a valuable resource for traditional medicine and a potential crop for sustainable food systems.
properties: Phaseolus trilobatus, also known as the three-lobed bean, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help in the treatment of various health conditions. constituents: Phaseolus trilobatus contains various active constituents, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids. These compounds have been reported to possess antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The flavonoids in Phaseolus trilobatus may also have potential anti-cancer and cardio-protective effects. preparations: Phaseolus trilobatus, also known as desert pod or three-lobed bean, is a wild bean species native to the Southwestern United States and Mexico. side_effects: Phaseolus trilobatus is a wild bean native to North America, and like many legumes, it may cause gastrointestinal upset in some individuals due to its high fiber and lectin content. It's also possible that the plant's seeds or pods may trigger allergic reactions in people with sensitivities. intro: Phaseolus trilobatus, also known as trilobe bean or three-leafed bean, is a perennial legume native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic effects. The bioactive constituents of Phaseolus trilobatus include glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Phaseolus trilobatus is often prepared as a decoction or tea, as well as being used in tinctures and infusions, to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of diabetes.
